entry.title: Learn How to Build Analytical Queries Using ABAP CDS
entry.content:
The data foundation for SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics is the Virtual Data Model (VDM). The VDM is built using ABAP CDS views that form a stack. The stack starts at the bottom with dimension and fact CDS views reading from ABAP tables. The next layer is the cube that combine the dimensions and measures. Finally we have the analytical queries that are built using a special type of ABAP CDS view called a CDS analytical projection view and they sit at the top layer.

SAP provide thousands of CDS views for all the levels of the stack, out-of-the-box and for all application areas. So why would you want to learn how to build them if SAP already provide them?

Firstly, it is helpful to be able to explore the SAP-supplied CDS views and make sense of them. To do this you need to understand the special syntax that is used. Secondly, you may want to develop your own custom CDS views from scratch for consumption by the analytics tools of SAP S/4HANA. And thirdly, SAP Business Data Cloud consumes ABAP CDS views to generate Data Products. So being familiar with this technology is a very useful skill to have in a BDC project.

In the learning journey Discovering SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics we dip our toe in the water and introduce you to the CDS views of the virtual data model. But we don't go into detail.

For those who want to really dig deep into this topic we developed the learning journey: 
Developing Analytical Queries using ABAP CDS 

In this learning journey you will learn how to to create your own analytical queries using ABAP CDS from scratch. There are lots of videos and code snippets that show you how to express your query requirements.

We even provide you with all the code that is used in the learning journey so you can follow along in your own system. 

As well as the learning journey, we provide the following Live Sessions that bring to life various aspects of analytical query building.

Develop Queries using CDS Analytical Projection Views 

Building Cubes using ABAP CDS Views 

Developing Hierarchies using ABAP CDS 

From Cube to Analytical App in 10 minutes Using the ADT Generator 

We highly recommend you attend these planned Live Sessions (or watch the recordings) to help you quickly develop your skills and to ask questions and learn more about planned enhancements.

Developing analytical queries using ABAP CDS is a hot topic. Many customers need help and this is why we created this learning journey. Remember, the virtual data model of SAP S/4HANA depends on ABAP CDS views. Understanding how they work will ensure you are a much sought after member of your SAP project team.

entry.title: Question bout how to install doc AI and chose editon and version and question about pricing
entry.content:
Hi

I hope the community can help me clarify two Document AI questions.

1) First question is about installation and how to choose edition and version

The documentation is talking a lot about functionality limited to the premium edition, but I have found absolutely no documentation about how to get a premium edition installed.

Basically all I can find about installation is below page. It says noting about base edition vs Premium edition and it says noting about how to install v1 or v2. I assume that I have to choose edition and version?

As far as I can see the boosters are only for Free Tier, so also no help to get there.

2) My second question is about the pricing

I think the pricing is cryptic. Again the pricing is related to the edition and version. From the anti-overview in the documentation I see that base edition is only for version 1 and that premium edition is available in both flavours V1 and V2. So far so good although that primarily brings me back to the first question - how to choose edition and version during installation (Enabling).

I can also see that base edition and premium edition are both metered on blocks of 100 documents. The base editon is additionally metered on "compute hours" where the premium edition is metered on requests and users.

(Here I notice that Premium Edition/Version 1 does not have metering of compute hours and also no metering of "Requsts and Users" - only blocks of documents. I wonder if this is just an error in the documentation?)

So far so complicated and still no idea what the cost is.

The documentation also mention that a block of 100 documents cost about 20 EUR - sometimes a document counts for more than one document, but so far ok understandable and it seems to be a ok price.

1 Compute hour cost 1 EUR - yeehaa that's cheap
1 template activation cost 5 compute hours - ok, then forget about the "yeehaa that's cheap"
1 transaction(document) cost 1 compute second - yeehaa that's very cheep
1 compute hour = 500 compute seconds - wait is an hour not 3600 seconds  - ok, then forget about the "yeehaa that's very cheap"

Then in the middle of the metering and pricing info is a insert stating that by HR the pricing is different :

Back on track with the pricing for the premium edition.

With premium version 1 and 2 now the minimum block size is 500 documents a month and the minimum price is 300 EUR, so this raise the price per document from 20 cent to 60 cent  with a little discount if you load extremely many documents.

 

Finally the premium edition version 2 has an additional metering/pricing for " Requests and Users". If I try to add this to the pricing formular, my head begins to spin, I forget all I just learned and I have to start all over again.

Currently I have no idea how to choose edition and version and I have no idea what the cost would be if I managed to choose a edition and version. The boosters only help to install a free edition, but that is not really what we need.

Best Regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Re: FB50 Display only
entry.content: Selecting 'Display' triggers transaction 'FB03', so don't allow user to 'FB50' but to 'FB03'.

entry.title: Re: FB50 Display only
entry.content:
FB50 uses multiple authorization objects. Restricting only one (e.g., ACTVT = 03 in F_BKPF_BUK) is not enough. The user’s role may include another object (like F_BKPF_BLA with ACTVT = 01) that enables posting. SAP checks all relevant objects during posting, so if any allow posting, the transaction goes through.

Solution

  • Run SU53 after the user posts — this shows which authorizations were checked and granted.
  • Check the role in PFCG and adjust all relevant objects:
    • Ensure F_BKPF_AKT is set to ACTVT = 03 only.
    • Remove ACTVT = 01/02 from other objects like F_BKPF_BLA.
  • If you want the user to only display documents:
    • Assign them the role with FB03 (Display Document) instead of FB50.
    • Or restrict FB50 completely and give them display-only transactions.

Normally, all the above activity would be carried out by Security team and you can check with them

entry.title: Re: Configuring SAML 2.0 Authentication for your Secure Login Server
entry.content:
Hi Kalyan,

You can use our SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI solution. Here the authentication is based on an identity provider. By default, this is SAP's Identity Authentication Service but you can also use a third-party identity provider such as Microsoft Entra ID. After successful authentication, the SAP Secure Login Service provisions a short-lived X.509 certificate which can then be used by the Secure Login Client for SSO (SNC) to your ABAP-based systems.

You will find more information about the SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI in SAP Community here:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/single-sign-on

Best regards,

Martina

entry.title: Re: Disable custom action button when a field value chang...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Can you please try the following procedure suggested by AI?

To dynamically enable or disable a custom action button based on a field value change in an SAP Fiori application, you can use the attachChange event on the field in question. This event allows you to listen for changes to the field value and execute a function when the change occurs.

Here is a step-by-step guide to achieve this:

  1. Identify the Field: Determine the field whose value change should trigger the enabling or disabling of the button.

  2. Attach Change Event: Use the attachChange method to listen for changes on the field. This can be done in the onInit method of your controller.

    onInit: function() {
    var oField = this.byId('yourFieldId');
    oField.attachChange(this.onFieldValueChange, this);
},

onFieldValueChange: function(oEvent) {
    var sValue = oEvent.getParameter('newValue');
    var bEnabled = sValue !== ''; // Enable if not blank
    this.byId('idUploadAtt').setEnabled(bEnabled);
}

  3. Update Button State: In the onFieldValueChange function, check the new value of the field. If the value is blank, set the button’s enabled property to false; otherwise, set it to true.

  4. Test: Ensure that the button’s state changes as expected when the field value is modified.

This approach ensures that the button’s enabled state is dynamically updated whenever the field value changes, not just during routing or mode changes.

Kind regards,
Henrike

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
Hello Tobias,
we use simplification items as technical means to determine compatibility package (CP) usage in SAP S/4HANA systems.
Actually, we use them for this purpose since the introduction of the simplification item check framework in 2017.

All simplification items used for compatibility packages are assigned to category "non-strategic-function". 
So, if you have chosen the new CP feature in report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK you will only see CP related items.
For the upgrade use case, you will also find "normal" simplification items with other categories describing deprecations or incompatible changes. If you run a Readiness Check for Upgrade, the simplification items will be displayed in separate tiles in the dashboard.

The fact that the local report does not show the related CP IDs (the 100s and 400s IDs) is a known gap. We currently think about how to mitigate this issue.

Best regards,
Stefan
SAP S/4HANA Product Management

entry.title: Re: Trading partner determination for PGI Material accoun...
entry.content:
This “invalid trading partner” error during PGI in intercompany sales is a classic sign that the trading partner assignment between company codes is inconsistent.

How Trading Partner is determined
Trading partner (field RASSC) is derived from the company code master data (transaction OBY6). Each company code has a field “Trading Partner” where you assign the partner company code. During PGI, the system posts inventory and COGS entries. If the delivering plant belongs to a different company code, the system looks up the trading partner from the company code master of the plant’s company code. If that trading partner points to a different sales org/company code than expected, you’ll see the mismatch you described.

Why you are seeing the wrong Trading Partner
Your sales org 1234 belongs to company code A, but the delivering plant 5678 belongs to company code B. At PGI, the system posts inventory for company code B. The trading partner is pulled from company code B’s master data. If company code B’s trading partner is set incorrectly (e.g., pointing to 3456 instead of 1234), the accounting document will show the “invalid trading partner” error.

How to Check
Go to transaction OBY6 (Company Code Global Data). For the delivering plant’s company code (5678’s company code), check the Trading Partner field. Verify that it points to the correct company code of the selling company (1234’s company code). Do the same for the selling company code — both sides must have consistent trading partner assignments.

entry.title: SAP Testing in 2026: Agentic AI and Quality Engineering for S/4HANA & BTP
entry.content:
Quality Engineering Transformation in S/4HANA and BTP Programs

Quality engineering integrated with S/4HANA project architecture is the new norm. It is no longer feasible for a company to simply convert to S/4HANA; they are reinventing their finance, logistics, manufacturing, and procurement processes while transitioning custom logic to BTP and running side-by-side applications. With each architectural choice comes the added complexity of testing for data integrity, service integration, authorization models, and performance characteristics.

In this context, testing begins well before a system is built. Quality engineers are involved in the solution design phase to assess migration paths, extensibility patterns, and potential integration risks. Data conversion strategies should be validated not only for their structural integrity, but also for their business relevance as they relate to S/4HANA’s simplified data model. This can be particularly challenging with historical data. During the build phase, regular configuration changes and transport movements require continuous regression testing rather than milestone-based testing.

Several structural changes define SAP quality engineering at this stage:

  • Testing scope is increasingly derived from business criticality rather than transaction count. Processes with financial exposure or regulatory impact receive deeper validation.
  • BTP extensions require testing beyond SAP GUI transactions. API behavior, event messaging, and cloud security controls must be validated under real usage conditions.
  • Performance and stability testing have shifted earlier in the lifecycle, particularly for hybrid scenarios where S/4HANA interacts with external platforms.

Within this framework, SAP teams are emerging as a practical response to scale. Autonomous systems analyze all configuration changes and provide valuable recommendations for which specific tests should be conducted to achieve maximum test coverage. The SAP Artificial Intelligence team partners with the Quality Engineers to ensure that the intelligence used in testing aligns with the test's business intent. This leads to testing that adapts rather than exhausts test execution, resulting in reduced execution overhead and improved reliability of the S/4HANA and BTP landscapes.

Agentic AI and the Evolution of SAP Testing Models

The adoption of agentic AI introduces a fundamentally different operating model for SAP testing. Automation systems that have been traditionally used, either defined by static logic elements and pre-written scripts or operate in just a 'set and forget' manner. The way that agentic AI is built, on the other hand, is to learn from the outcomes that occur, identify the patterns associated with those outcomes, and act on its own within a pre-defined set of constraints. Automated processes that rely on predetermined scripts to run a series of tests will not work well in SAP systems, as they are constantly changing and closely interrelated.

AI agents in SAP testing environments are capable of monitoring transport activity, configuration updates, and integration changes. When a test fails, these agents evaluate the execution context rather than simply logging an error. In this case, they can determine whether the test failure is due to data inconsistencies, authorization issues, or delays caused by upstream integrations. By reasoning the error context, agents are much more accurate at prioritizing and classifying defects, reducing the need for rework.

Common applications of agentic AI in SAP quality engineering include:

  • Dynamic regression planning where agents adjust test scope based on recent system changes rather than executing full regression cycles.
  • Intelligent failure clustering that groups related defects and identifies systemic issues instead of isolated symptoms.
  • Context-aware test data generation that aligns with real business scenarios, improving validation depth for complex S/4HANA processes.

These capabilities also reshape team structures. Agentic SAP teams operate with a shared responsibility model, where quality engineers define validation objectives, and AI systems handle execution level decisions. SAP AI teams provide governance, ensuring transparency and auditability of autonomous actions. Over time, these systems accumulate knowledge from prior releases, enabling predictive insights that anticipate failure patterns before they impact production.

As enterprises evolve to develop Agentic AI Services, there’s an ever-increasing demand from companies that want to create scalable solutions to incorporate into their existing SAP landscapes, while also minimizing the amount of manual work. If properly implemented, SAP testing programs may realize tangible improvements in release confidence, defect containment, and operating efficiency - all without sacrificing an enterprise’s ability to maintain control.

Conclusion

The defining characteristics of SAP testing in 2026 are depth, continuity, and intelligence. The quality engineering practices required for S/4HANA and BTP programs require an understanding of the technical architecture and business context. Active testing has moved beyond a final check to become an inherent part of the testing discipline, influencing design, observing how it is executed, and facilitating continual improvement in SAP environments.

The rise of agentic AI in SAP testing represents a natural progression in this evolution. By enabling autonomous reasoning and adaptive execution, AI agents (e.g., chatbots) within SAP ecosystems enable organizations to manage the complexity of their operations without increasing testing overhead. As SAP AI teams add these capabilities to SAP, and enterprises use agentic AI services, quality engineering becomes predictive, reactive, and aligned with overarching enterprise transformation goals. In this environment, AI used for SAP testing is no longer an experiment but an operational requirement for maintaining reliable, scalable SAP systems.

entry.title: Re: Question bout how to install doc AI and chose editon...
entry.content:
Good questions and you're definitely not alone in being confused by this, the docs can be a bit all over the place on this topic 😅

The key thing you're probably missing is that there's no separate "edition selection" step during setup. The edition and version are simply determined by which BTP service plan you pick when creating your service instance.

Since you want Premium Edition, forget the Booster as you noticed, it's really only meant for Free Tier. Instead, do it manually:

1. BTP Cockpit → your subaccount → Entitlements → Edit → Add Service Plans → search for SAP Document AI → add the `premium_edition` plan
2. Then go to Service Marketplace → SAP Document AI → Create Instance → pick `premium_edition` as plan

There's a step-by-step tutorial on SAP Developers that walks through exactly this (just swap the `free` plan for `premium_edition` wherever it mentions it):
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-aibus-dox-free-service-instance..html

And for the general Initial Setup, this is your go-to reference:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DOCUMENT_AI/5fa7265b9ff64d73bac7cec61ee55ae6/0d68dc0002f0484ba25f85f3170166e0.html

On the pricing, you've already mentioned most of it yourself! Given the complexity of the Premium V2 metering on top of everything else, I'd honestly just reach out to your SAP account manager at this point. They'll be able to give you a concrete cost estimate based on your actual document volumes, which is going to be way more useful than trying to calculate it from the documentation.

entry.title: Re: 'Function SUMGT is unknown' error in Custom Analytica...
entry.content:
I can see that you have used SUMGT in calculated measure. This is not allowed in Custom analytical query. You can use GRAND_TOTAL as an alternative for SUMGT in this case.

To check the values that are allowed in calculated measure, please click on Functions Button to view the values that are available.

entry.title: Sales in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
This blog shares highlights on Sales in SAP Cloud ERP 2602.

  • Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): enablement of sales kits
  • Manage Sales Documents app: initial scope
  • Input assistance in the SAP Fiori app Manage Customer Returns – Version 2
  • Data determination in access for pricing
  • Propagating Custom Fields from Product Master Data to Sales Document Item
  • New Microsoft Excel Export Formats for Price Management
  • Flexible Proposal of Validity Dates for Sales Contracts
  • Key User Extensibility for Billing Document Scheduling
  • Automatic Creation of Sales Orders from Unstructured Data
  • Customer Returns for Third-Party Sales with Variant Configuration
  • No Refund Option for Customer Returns Involving Suppliers
  • Detection of Overlapping Condition Records in Price Management
  • Introduction of More Fields for the Customer Invoice – Send (B2B) SOAP API
  • Returns Order OData v4 API: Enhancements Such as Serial Number and Address
  • Configuration of Customer-Specific Sales Order Types Using Sales Order Templates in SAP Central Business Configuration
  • Ability to Read Pricing Elements with the Preliminary Billing Document OData V4 API
  • Additional Capabilities of the Sales Order OData V4 API
  • BAdI for Customer-Specific Checks in Preliminary Billing Documents
  • Additional Capability for the Manage Sales Contracts - Version 2 App
  • Sales Contract OData V4 API - Additional Capability

Watch my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): enablement of sales kits

​Sales kit (Sales BOM) processing can now be enabled in advanced intercompany sales. This feature increase operational efficiency and reduce complexity by using predefined products that consist of several components. This feature reduces manual effort and increase automation by using Monitor Value Chain to automatically post the intercompany purchase document and the intercompany sales order for inventory-controlled goods, and to monitor further and orchestrate the process.
 
The following graphic illustrates the business process.
 
Graphic: Advanced intercompany sales (5D2): enablement of sales kits

This capability lets a selling organization offer a sales kit while an affiliated delivering company fulfills it. A kit contains both inventory-controlled items and at least one non-inventory-controlled item; intercompany billing and stock movements apply only to the inventory-controlled components. The process supports multiple kit types already available in sell-from-stock, ranging from main-item level processing to bracket-style kits where sub-items carry pricing, inventory control, and delivery. When the delivering plant’s company code differs from the selling company’s, the advanced intercompany flow is triggered: the customer-facing sales order holds the full kit, while the intercompany PO, SO, and invoices include only the stock-managed sub-items. The outbound delivery shows the full kit, with goods movements on inventory-relevant sub-items, and the Value Chain Monitoring app automatically creates and orchestrates intercompany documents with end-to-end document flow visibility.

This enables multinational sales of complete kits without adding operational complexity, ensuring costs and revenues are correctly posted between affiliates for the inventory-managed components. Predefined multi-component products reduce manual steps and errors, while automation via Value Chain Monitoring minimizes effort and shortens cycle times. Supporting multiple kit types provides flexibility to fit varied pricing and fulfillment patterns, helping standardize cross-company operations and improve transparency. Overall, it boosts revenue opportunities with kits, streamlines intercompany processing, and enhances financial accuracy across delivering and selling entities.

For more information, see SAP Help Sales Kits in Advanced Intercompany Sales

The following table gives you an overview of the most important differences between the available ways of processing for items:

Table: Overview of Differences in Processing Sales Kit Items in Advanced Intercompany Sales

This video explains the concept.

 

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

Manage Sales Documents app: initial scope

The Manage Sales Document app is the central entry point to work with sales documents, initially covering Sales Orders and Sales Orders Without Charge. It provides rich, connected search across header, item, and schedule line levels, with refinements by common attributes and the option to add more fields, including the ability to search by one-time customer name. Users get context-appropriate actions at each level, can create different order types, and are routed to the right creation experience: the Fiori “Manage Sales Orders – Version 2” for supported types or the Web GUI for types not yet in Fiori. A highly requested grid-style experience is supported through flexible list personalization and the ability to export results to Excel or PDF.

The app consolidates tasks into a single, modern experience that preserves familiar workflows for VA05 users while expanding capabilities. The connected, multi-level search accelerates finding and acting on orders, reducing navigation overhead and time-to-resolution. Personalization and export features improve reporting, analysis, and handoffs to downstream processes, while context-aware navigation avoids disruption when certain document types still require the Web GUI. Together, these improvements enhance productivity, support real-world scenarios like one-time customers, and ease adoption by balancing continuity with innovation.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

Input assistance in the SAP Fiori app Manage Customer Returns – Version 2

The Manage Customer Returns - Version 2 app now provides real-time, AI-driven recommendations during returns order creation. After entering key context—like sales area and sold-to at header or product at item—the system suggests values such as return reasons, logistical follow-up activities, receiving plant, and even refund codes based on learned patterns from historical returns. Recommendations are ranked, with probabilities above 17% highlighted, and users can accept or adjust them before releasing the order. Activation can be turnkey if prerequisites are met: the Data Attribute Recommendation collection is set up and sufficient data volumes exist (roughly 3,000 eligible items in production and 1,000 in quality/test). Teams can also opt for manual training, disable specific scenarios, or cancel them as needed.

You will benefit from faster, more accurate data entry that reduces user effort and training time—especially for new or infrequent users—and improves process consistency. By leveraging historical patterns, the system helps standardize choices like follow-up activities and refund codes, lowering errors and rework and speeding customer resolutions. Governed activation and scenario control strike a balance between ease of adoption and enterprise oversight, making the capability scalable across different sales areas and product portfolios. Ultimately, the AI assistance shortens cycle times in returns handling, enhances customer satisfaction, and supports cleaner operational data for analytics and continuous improvement.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

Data determination in access for pricing

This release introduces the ability to create custom data fields and leverage them for data determination within pricing access sequences. The system can now identify and determine data that isn’t directly available in the business document, storing it in a dedicated structure (KOMPAZD) for use throughout subsequent pricing procedure steps. Key users can configure this data determination through access sequences defined by condition tables containing data fields, and create condition records where field values are determined based on factors like the product—eliminating the need to enhance the underlying document structure itself.

Organizations can now achieve more flexible and sophisticated pricing scenarios without modifying core business documents, streamlining implementation and maintenance efforts. This approach significantly reduces the volume of condition records needed, as data can be dynamically determined rather than explicitly stored across multiple record combinations. The result is a leaner, more maintainable pricing configuration that adapts to complex requirements while minimizing system overhead and administrative complexity.

Screenshot: Data determination in access for pricing

For more information, see: Extensibility: Data Determination in Access

 

Propagating Custom Fields from Product Master Data to Sales Document Item

Key users can now enable custom fields to automatically flow from product master data to sales document items through the Custom Fields app. When this business scenario is activated, the system automatically copies custom field values from the product master whenever a user creates a sales document item based on that product. Users retain the flexibility to modify these custom field values directly within the sales document item if needed.

This enhancement significantly streamlines the extensibility of sales documents by eliminating the need to manually enter custom field data across multiple touchpoints. Organizations can reduce complexity in maintaining custom fields across product master data and sales documents while improving overall efficiency in document processing and data consistency.

Screenshot: Propagating Custom Fields from Product Master Data to Sales Document Item

New Microsoft Excel Export Formats for Price Management

The Manage Prices – Sales app now supports exporting prices using enhanced Microsoft Excel formats, including proper "number" and "value" formatting where applicable. This means exported price data appears in native Excel formats rather than as plain text, making the data immediately usable for calculations and analysis.

Users can now leverage Excel's native functionalities directly on exported files without needing to reformat data manually. This improvement enhances productivity for pricing specialists who regularly work with exported price lists and need to perform calculations, create reports, or integrate pricing data with other business processes.

Screenshot: New Microsoft Excel Export Formats for Price Management

Flexible Proposal of Validity Dates for Sales Contracts

Organizations can now define rules for dynamically proposing valid-to dates on sales contracts. When creating a new sales contract, the system evaluates document header fields and applies specific calendar and offset rules to automatically propose appropriate validity dates. This leverages the configurable parameter and formula (CPF) framework for maximum flexibility.

This capability enables companies to tailor validity date management to their specific business requirements, reducing manual intervention and minimizing customer complaints caused by incorrect or incomplete validity dates during release order processing. The automation ensures consistency and accuracy across contract management processes.

Screenshot: Flexible Proposal of Validity Dates for Sales Contracts

Key User Extensibility for Billing Document Scheduling

Key users can now add up to five custom fields to the Schedule Billing Creation and Schedule Creation of Preliminary Billing Documents apps. Business users can then use these custom fields as selection parameters when scheduling billing document creation jobs, enabling precise filtering of which documents should be processed.

This extensibility empowers business users to tailor job selection criteria to their unique processes without requiring IT involvement or waiting for product enhancements. The precise filtering capability improves automation efficiency and ensures only the intended documents are processed during billing runs, increasing control and accuracy in billing operations.

Screenshot: Key User Extensibility for Billing Document Scheduling

Automatic Creation of Sales Orders from Unstructured Data

Internal sales representatives can now create sales orders directly from purchase order files in PDF or image formats. After uploading a file, the system automatically extracts relevant information into a sales order request and proposes field values—such as determining the sold-to party from extracted data. The sales order request can then be converted into a final sales order, with multilanguage support and individual training capabilities powered by SAP Document AI.

This automation dramatically increases sales force efficiency by eliminating manual data entry from paper or digital documents. Organizations can reduce processing time and human error while handling customer orders, allowing sales teams to focus on higher-value activities rather than administrative data transcription tasks.

Watch this demo to have a complete view of the feature.

 

Customer Returns for Third-Party Sales with Variant Configuration

Organizations using scope item 4R6 (Sales Processing using Third-Party with Variant Configuration) can now process returns of configurable materials from end customers. When handling these returns, users can select logistical follow-up activities to ship materials back to the third-party supplier, and the system automatically transfers the variant configuration from the returns order to the returns purchase order.

This enhancement completes the end-to-end process for handling returns in third-party variant configuration scenarios. Companies can now efficiently manage the entire lifecycle of configurable products sold through third parties, ensuring seamless coordination between customer-facing returns processes and supplier return logistics.

Screenshot: Customer Returns for Third-Party Sales with Variant Configuration

No Refund Option for Customer Returns Involving Suppliers

Customer returns can now be processed with a "No Refund" control option when the return involves shipping goods back to a supplier. This applies to logistical follow-up activities for shipping to supplier or direct shipment to supplier, supporting scenarios where non-refundable goods—such as free samples provided by third-party suppliers—are returned.

This capability streamlines return processing by providing a cleaner, simpler workflow for specific return scenarios. Organizations can handle non-refundable returns more efficiently and accurately, improving operational speed while enhancing the overall customer experience through transparent and appropriate refund handling.

Screenshot: No Refund Option for Customer Returns Involving Suppliers

Detection of Overlapping Condition Records in Price Management

The Manage Prices – Sales app now displays a warning message when users create a condition record that overlaps with existing ones. An overlap occurs when the validity period of a new condition record conflicts with other records sharing the same condition type and price-relevant attributes. Previously, the system silently adjusted existing records without notification.

This proactive alerting improves collaboration among pricing specialists by making them aware of potential conflicts before they occur. Teams can review overlapping records and their validity periods, make informed adjustments, and maintain cleaner, more accurate pricing data across the organization.

Screenshot: Detection of Overlapping Condition Records in Price Management

Introduction of More Fields for the Customer Invoice – Send (B2B) SOAP API

The Customer Invoice – Send (B2B) SOAP API has been enhanced with additional fields at both the billing document header and item levels. New fields added to the PaymentTerms node include CustomerPaymentTermsName and payment term descriptions (PaymentTerm1Description, PaymentTerm2Description, PaymentTerm3Description). At the item level, the API now supports dates from preceding documents such as CustomerPurchaseOrderDate, DeliveryDate, and SalesDocumentCreationDate, along with reference information including SalesDocument, SalesDocumentItem, ReferenceSDDocument, ReferenceSDDocumentCategory, ReferenceSDDocumentItem, and CountryOfOrigin.

This enhancement ensures that important information is automatically transmitted to end customers through the API, supporting critical activities such as tracking key performance indicators and complying with specific regulations. Organizations can now provide more comprehensive invoice data to their B2B customers without manual intervention, improving data completeness and facilitating smoother downstream processes for both parties.

 

Returns Order OData v4 API: Enhancements Such as Serial Number and Address

The Returns Order OData v4 API (Customer Return A2X) has been enhanced with several key capabilities to support more comprehensive returns processing. Users can now leverage various new fields in the header and item entities, including purchase order type for ship-to party. Additionally, the API supports managing serial numbers for relevant returns order items and managing address data for business partners within returns orders.

These enhancements increase returns order processing efficiency for business integration scenarios. Organizations can now exchange more complete and accurate returns information with external systems, reducing manual data handling and enabling smoother automated workflows between SAP and connected applications or partners.

 

Configuration of Customer-Specific Sales Order Types Using Sales Order Templates in SAP Central Business Configuration

Organizations can now leverage SAP’s preconfigured best practice sales order types as templates to create customer-specific sales order type variants. A dedicated user application within SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) enables users to create and manage these custom sales order types, while also providing the ability to adjust the assignment of business processes to ensure consistency when introducing new customer-specific configurations.

This capability delivers a streamlined and flexible sales order process by allowing organizations to configure tailor-made sales order types that match their unique requirements. Internal sales representatives can operate more efficiently by utilizing a targeted subset of preconfigured business process capabilities, while the organization reduces overall configuration and maintenance effort through easy creation and deployment of customer-specific variants with minimal customization.

 

Ability to Read Pricing Elements with the Preliminary Billing Document OData V4 API

The Preliminary Billing Document OData V4 API has been enhanced with the capability to read pricing elements. Users can now retrieve pricing element data at both the header and item levels, including raw condition types and subtotal lines associated with preliminary billing document headers and items.

This enhancement provides comprehensive visibility into pricing structures within preliminary billing documents. Organizations can access detailed pricing element data effortlessly, improving transparency and understanding of how prices are calculated. The ability to retrieve condition types and subtotal lines facilitates accurate analysis and reporting, enabling finance and sales teams to better validate pricing accuracy and support downstream billing processes.

 

Additional Capabilities of the Sales Order OData V4 API

The Sales Order OData V4 API has been enhanced with significant new capabilities for processing sales orders. Users can now leverage multilevel variant configuration with more flexible identification methods for subinstances, access partner address data including phone number fields, and retrieve additional pricing element and sales order item fields. The API supports creating sales orders with reference to other sales documents, including the ability to change product parameters during this process. Additional capabilities include automatic assignment of sales contract items when creating sales orders, processing partner address data with one-time customer support, and retriggering pricing at both sales order and sales order item levels.

These enhancements unlock new revenue streams and business opportunities through smooth integration with external webshops, quoting solutions, and analytical engines. Organizations can simplify development and streamline maintenance by eliminating the need for multiple APIs, such as legacy Sales OData V2 APIs. The improvements lower total cost of ownership through robust, automated integration for order taking and processing, while harmonizing the integration experience by leveraging the latest features of the ABAP RESTful application programming model. Implementation timelines are accelerated by fully utilizing advanced OData V4 protocol features, reducing effort when setting up new integration scenarios.

Sales Order OData V4 API - Multi Level Variant Configuration - Soft Identification

Identification Options for Sub-Instances in Multi Level Variant Configurations

You now have different options to identify sub-instances in multi level variant configurations during create requets:

You can use one property or a combination of properties from the entity Variant Configuration Instance as follow:

  • IdentifierBOMItem

  • BillOfMaterialItemNumber and Product

  • IdentifierBOMItem, BillOfMaterialItemNumber, and Product

Sales Order OData V4 API - Field Enhancements (Address, Pricing Element, Item Entities)

Address Fields
  • For API_SALESORDER, new partner address fields are added to the Header Partner and Item Partner entities.
  • For API_SALESORDERSIMULATION: address fields are added to the Header Partner and Item Partner entities.

Pricing Element Fields
Fields have been added to the SalesOrderPricingElement and SalesOrderItemPricingElement entities of API_SALESORDER and API_SALESORDERSIMULATION

Item Entities
Fields have been added Sales Order Item entity of API_SALESORDER and API_SALESORDERSIMULATION.
 
Purchase Confirmation Status Check
The following entities have been updated for API_SALESORDER and API_SALESORDERSIMULATION:
  • Sales Order Header
  • Sales Order Item

Entity Update
Properties have been added to the Sales Order Header entity of API_SALESORDERSIMULATION
  • Sales Order (A2X, OData V4)
  • Sales Order - Simulate (A2X, OData V4)

 

BAdI for Customer-Specific Checks in Preliminary Billing Documents

A new business add-in (BAdI) enables organizations to implement custom logic for performing checks and blocks on preliminary billing documents. Using the BAdI Check of Data in Preliminary Billing Documents (SD_BIL_PBD_ACTION_CHECK) through the Custom Logic app, users can leverage header and item fields along with custom fields to block the finalization of preliminary billing documents, prevent the creation of billing documents from preliminary billing documents, and block the "Auto Finalize" action—instead creating the preliminary billing document with an "In Progress" status. When a block occurs, the system provides users with an error message containing relevant information.

This capability allows organizations to establish country-specific and industry-specific rules to monitor and restrict processes related to preliminary billing documents. Companies can manage complex billing procedures more effectively, particularly in the professional services industry, ensuring compliance with specific regulations and internal policies while improving overall user satisfaction through controlled and transparent billing workflows.

 

Additional Capability for the Manage Sales Contracts - Version 2 App

The Manage Sales Contracts - Version 2 SAP Fiori app has been enhanced with several new maintenance capabilities for comprehensive sales contract management. Users can now maintain contract-related partner functions including sold-to party and ship-to party directly within the app. Additional supported features include authorized partner checking rule maintenance, price list type maintenance, and sales contract description maintenance. The app also introduces new search help functionality for product hierarchy and product hierarchy node, enabling users to more efficiently locate and assign hierarchical product information within sales contracts.

These enhancements increase productivity by enabling users to maintain the majority of sales contract information directly in the modern SAP Fiori app without requiring duplicate effort in legacy transactions. Organizations benefit from improved user friendliness through the modern UI capabilities, streamlining the contract management experience and reducing the need to switch between different interfaces to complete contract-related tasks.

 

Sales Contract OData V4 API - Additional Capability

The advanced version of the Sales Contract OData V4 API has been enhanced with additional capabilities to create, read, and delete sales contracts and their entities. New supported fields include sales description on the sales contract header and price list type on the sales contract header, expanding the scope of contract data that can be managed through the API.

These enhancements improve holistic contract management by enabling seamless integration of sales contracts with external systems. Organizations benefit from improved performance through the new features offered in the ABAP RESTful application programming model, such as choosing between synchronous or asynchronous request processing and opting for minimal responses containing only the key of created entities. The API supports direct filtering of subentities and leverages a future-proof, stable architecture consistent across objects. Implementation is streamlined as no additional effort is required to set up a communication arrangement—the Sales Contract OData V4 API is included directly in the existing communication arrangement, saving valuable setup time.

  • Price list type enablement in Sales Contract OData V4 API

    A new property PriceListType has been added to the SalesContract entity of the Sales Contract (A2X, OData V4) (API_SalesContract)API. You can use this property to identify a price list or other condition type (for example, a surcharge or discount).

  • Sales description enablement in Sales Contract OData V4 API

    A new property SalesContractDescription has been added to the SalesContract entity of the Sales Contract (A2X, OData V4) (API_SalesContract) API. You can use this property as a text description of a sales contract that can be used as a search term for locating the sales contract.

 

Feel free to follow us in the SAP community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates.

entry.title: Here’s what everyone is talking about after the Network Success Day in Prague
entry.content:
“No business does business alone.”

That message set the tone as SAP kicked off 2026 with its first Network Success Day (NSD) event in Prague. Suppliers, buyers, and SAP experts came together to exchange practical tips and advice, explore new SAP product innovations, and improve collaboration across SAP Business Network.

Following successful events in Amsterdam and Berlin in 2025, the strong turnout in Prague confirmed one thing: the appetite for more SAP Business Network expertise and guidance continues to grow.

If you couldn’t attend the event, here’s what you missed – and why you should think about joining us next time.

What makes SAP Network Success Days different? 

The NSD event was more than standard presentations. Attendees joined “meet the expert” sessions, intimate breakout discussions, live Q&A conversations, and informal networking events. Suppliers and buyers had opportunities to share their real SAP Business Network challenges and honest feedback, as well as learn from industry peers facing similar business issues.

See SAP Business Network on SAP BTP in action

A major focus in Prague was the upgrade of SAP Business Network to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). SAP Business Network has always been one place for collaboration across multiple business areas, and now, with SAP BTP as its foundation, the network becomes much more than that.

For suppliers, it means greater scalability and performance through cloud infrastructure, improved integrations with SAP and third-party systems, and embedded AI and analytics for smarter decision-making. It will also strengthen compliance and data sovereignty and provide a unified user experience and safe extensibility with open APIs and low-code tools.

This isn’t simply a system upgrade. It improves SAP Business Network, allowing suppliers to scale operations, respond faster to changes, and support new business requirements more easily.

Keep an eye on our Community page for upcoming updates on what SAP Business Network on BTP means for suppliers. In the meantime, get a snapshot of the key benefits and the new user interface discussed in Prague by watching this video. And, access a more in-depth look at what’s new in this extensive overview for suppliers .

E-Invoicing: From compliance to competitive advantage

E-invoicing also dominated discussions at this NSD, and for good reason. With continuous transaction control (CTC) models, EU VAT in the digital age (VIDA) reforms, and expanding global mandates, regulatory complexity is increasing, and it extends beyond invoices to impact procurement, tax, payments, and finance documents. Attendees learned how SAP is responding to these changes with a unified, automated invoice management strategy, enhanced by AI, global compliance support through SAP Business Network, and seamless Procure-to-Pay collaboration across ERP systems.

The key takeaway is that e-invoicing readiness is no longer optional; it’s a must for reducing risk, improving efficiency, and strengthening trading partner relationships. Find out more in this e-invoice strategy documentation.

Better collaboration across SAP Business Network

Other sessions and discussions throughout the two days covered AI use cases in procurement and supply chains, improving supplier onboarding and adoption and better logistics collaboration with carriers. The bottom line? Data, automation, and a connected B2B network enable efficient collaboration.

Where open dialogue meets SAP innovation

The Prague NSD event was a space for transparent dialogue and firsthand insight into what SAP is actively doing to help buyers and suppliers thrive on SAP Business Network.

Across two days, the atmosphere struck a unique balance, with informal conversations taking place alongside in-depth strategy discussions. This gave attendees the freedom to probe, challenge, and raise the topics they felt deserved more attention from SAP teams. This open approach not only encouraged learning but also reinforced the importance of shared experiences and emphasized that innovation in the network comes from both SAP’s vision and the questions and feedback of its community. For anyone looking to understand the future of procurement, supplier collaboration, and network-driven efficiency, NDC events remain an unmissable chance to connect, learn, and influence the conversation in person.

SAP would also like to thank everyone who attended the event and contributed to its success.

Stay connected and get involved

Be sure to follow this SAP Business Network Community for more updates and upcoming NSD dates. And don’t forget to share your questions and feedback in the comments section of this blog post.

entry.title: Re: Automatically exporting invoice data to spreadsheet
entry.content:
One alternative in Fiori side, which would avoid MIR5, would be to use app Supplier Invoices List.

Once you get the invoice results, you can directly export data to spreadsheet format.

If you're requesting for an automatic way of exporting data, then that scenario would depend on a missing functionality, as a separate program would need to be involved.
You can suggest it as a new feature by contacting SAP through the steps from KBA 3507575 - Customer Influence channel for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Procurement

entry.title: Thrive XM Index - SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Dear SAP SuccessFactors Experts,

Is anyone using Thrive XM Index in any of your clients? I activated it from the Upgrade Center , and the tile was not visible in Manage Home Page -> Quick Actions.

Upon referring to the below KBA 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3571747/E

The KBA says the Tile was deprecated due to Low Usage. But the upgrade is still visible in Recommended Upgrades section in the Upgrade Center.

Its better to also be removed from the Upgrade center if its deprecated , so confusions can be avoided considering it appearing under Recommended Upgrades.

March 2026 sessions are outlined in this blog with brief descriptions.

Also get an insight on country- and deployment-specific SAP solution value accelerators using Process Navigator, different deployment options, countries, and localization requirements.

Goals:

Goals:

  • Prepare for S/4HANA migration using actionable data insights.

                                      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

✔️SAP Signavio Adoption Starter:  Process Insights for Business – Finance [English and Chinese].  Register Here.  This offering helps you to adopt and consume the capability and usability of the SAP Signavio Process Insights to enable business users to run in-depth process analysis. This session focuses on processes in Finance. 

Goals:

  • Understand key financial process data and its implications.

                                      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

✔️SAP Signavio Adoption Starter:  Process Insights for Business – Sales [English, Spanish, and Portuguese].  Register Here.  This offering helps you to adopt and consume the capability and usability of the SAP Signavio Process Insights to enable business users to run in-depth process analysis. Learn How to analyze your Lead-to-Cash process with SAP Signavio Process Insights.

Goals:

Learn How to analyze your Plan-to-Fulfill process with SAP Signavio Process Insights.

                                  ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Stay tuned each month to learn what courses will be offered next.

Regards,
Leean De Loria - Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps.

entry.title: Custom code migration to SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities
entry.content:
Custom code migration during system conversion to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is usually complex and time-consuming process. The whole custom code developed in the SAP ECC systems since years must be assessed and analysed for SAP S/4HANA and finally adapted after system conversion.

Customers often face challenges to understand S/4HANA simplifications and their relation to the custom code due to scattered documentation across various channels like SAP simplification notes, SAP Help, blog posts. To find an answer to the specific SAP S/4HANA custom code migration question, one needs to go through multiple sources of information.

The IT project managers have a tough time to carry out the custom code migration projects on time and within budget because they experience difficulties during execution of their tasks like defining custom code migration scope, getting high-level understanding of custom code issues, creating effort estimations and project plans and finding the right development experts for adaptation. Ability to meet a decision whether to migrate custom code to S/4HANA or not assumes understanding of its business purpose which is quite challenging primarily due to the absence of documentation for legacy code. Custom Code Migration app on SAP BTP can execute custom code analysis for S/4HANA readiness, but S/4HANA comes along with more than 430 simplification items which affect custom code. Thus, to get at least a high-level understanding of issues reported by the Custom Code Migration app to assign appropriate work packages to custom code migration developers a project manager needs to deep dive into these lengthy simplification items and provided cookbooks, which is time- and effort-consuming activity.

Along with this custom code migration developers, who finally must adapt the code for S/4HANA need to understand the old legacy code in all details: not only the business logic, but also the technical processing flow which is quite tedious task due to missing documentation. ABAP test cockpit (ATC) in ABAP development tools for Eclipse can check custom code for S/4HANA readiness, but developers still invest high effort into studying related simplification items and cookbooks to understand the issues in detail. The ATC issues in custom code should be fixed ideally with a high automation rate, but the quick fixes provided for S/4HANA ATC issues in ABAP development tools for Eclipse can support about 40%-60% of the typical S/4HANA simplification use cases like conflicts with MATNR field extension or missing ORDER BY in SELECTs, the remaining ATC issues must be fixed manually by developers.

To support IT project managers and developers during custom code migration projects to speed up the analysis and adaptation tasks and enhance productivity the AI-based skills were delivered for custom code management tools within SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities. This will shorten the custom code migration projects timelines, reduce project risks and enhance capacity to meet deadlines and budget constraints.

Let’s look at them in detail.

AI-based custom code migration support for IT project managers

Starting with SAP BTP ABAP environment 2602 AI-based custom code analysis support for IT project managers is integrated in the Custom Code Migration app. To use it the SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities must be activated in your SAP BTP ABAP environment system. See the SAP note 3571857.

Understand legacy code in the AI-based Custom Code Migration app

After execution of remote SAP S/4HANA readiness ATC check run over the custom code in the ECC system with the Custom Code Migration app in SAP BTP ABAP environment it is now possible under the Analyze Findings view to select ABAP program or ABAP class in the Findings table and use Code Explain capability to get explanation of its business purpose and processing logic.

You will get short and clear AI-based explanation of the code in the pop-up window, which will support your migration decision. Subsequently you can use Explain business purpose in detail and Explain processing logic in detail options to get more details to deepen the clarification.

Finally, you can use Scope part of the Custom Code Migration app to add or remove the object from scope based on your decision.

Understand S/4HANA ATC issues in the AI-based Custom Code Migration app

After execution of remote SAP S/4HANA readiness ATC check run over the custom code in the ECC system with the Custom Code Migration app in SAP BTP ABAP environment it is now possible under the Analyze Findings view to select an ATC issue in the Findings table and use ATC Explain capability to get an explanation of an S/4HANA related ATC finding. The explanation is provided in the pop-up window along with the proposed step-by-step resolution and contains also referenced S/4HANA simplification materials like simplification notes or cookbooks. Referenced documents are offered as clickable links within the AI-based response for navigation to further details.

This will enable you to grasp the root cause quickly without having to read through numerous SAP S/4HANA simplification information materials.

AI-based support for custom code migration developer in ABAP development tools for Eclipse

Starting with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 AI-based custom code analysis and adaptation support for developers is provided in ABAP development tools for Eclipse within SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities. To find out how to activate it see the blog post SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025.

NOTE: Code Explain for module pools and ABAP classes along with the deep dive explain (code in INCLUDEs and called methods outside of the source code object) will be delivered with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS01.

Using Docs Chat for questions around custom code and S/4HANA simplifications

In most cases you as a custom code migration developer before starting with your adaptation activities first need to gain general understanding of S/4HANA simplifications related to your functional area of expertise to get an overview as well as deep dive into SAP standard changes relevant to your custom code.

Docs Chat capability utilizes SAP simplification notes, related technical information and SAP Help documentation to deliver based on this extensive input quick and accurate answers.

Select /docs topic in the Joule chat window and ask your question, for example “What are the data model changes in Sales and Distribution?”. Joule will list all technical changes along with affected objects including also reference links for further information. You can also ask follow- up questions for your query.

Now for example after finding out that VBUK and VBUP tables were eliminated in S/4HANA and knowing that this has direct impact on your custom code, you can ask Joule ”How can references to VBUK and VBUP be eliminated in my code?”. Joule will deliver short and concrete answer providing code proposals considering different use cases based on the information provided in the extensive cookbook about the VBUK and VBUP adaption.

Understanding of old legacy code

Generally, you as a custom code migration developer starting with your adaptation activities need to understand the old legacy code, which must be analysed and adapted for S/4HANA. Thereby you are confronted with the following problems: the code has been implemented many years ago, often expands over multiple pages, has very little or no documentation, the author of the code is not available any more for questions and so on. Thus you have a tedious and long-time task to digest this code to verify if it is still needed from the business perspective and understand it’s implementation logic for the adaptation.

The Explain for ABAP report and Explain for module pools as interactive Dynpro-based applications capabilities significantly speed up and make more accurate the task of understanding of old legacy code.

Just select an old ABAP report in the Project Explorer and use context menu Joule ->Explain. Joule will explain its business purpose and processing logic and will list all called objects. You can further deep dive into details using Explain business purpose in detail or/and Explain processing logic in detail features in the Joule chat window or ask Joule to explain called objects.

In the example below the business purpose of this custom ABAP program is to upload customer email addresses, which length exceeds 50 characters since the standard ABAP program cannot handle it, and this custom functionality will be still required in the S/4HANA system.

Therefore, you as developer can for example follow up on the details of processing logic to understand the program better for the upcoming adaptation.

You can also select the source code snippet in the editor and ask Joule to explain it:

Here is one example of the module pool explanation by Joule. In addition to the general overview and business purpose Joule describes user flow, which is very important for screen-based Dynpro applications to follow on the screen logic along with technical details for the processing logic of the screens (PBO/PAI) comprising deep-dive into INCLUDEs and Dynpro logic:

Understanding of S/4HANA related ATC findings

The basis for all custom code adaptation activities is the execution of S/4HANA readiness checks with ATC over custom code in your S/4HANA system in ABAP development tools for Eclipse. The ATC check result contains the list of S/4HANA incompatibility issues in custom code, which must be adapted, explaining the error and providing as a reference the link to the corresponding simplification information. The custom code migration developer needs to read through it to find out how to apply it to solve the error in the particular custom code object, which is time-consuming task.

The Explain ATC finding capability offers clear and short explanation of the S/4HANA related issue in custom code based on information provided in S/4HANA simplification items and cookbooks along with step-by-step recommendation on how to solve the issue.

After execution of the ATC check run with S/4HANA readiness check variant S4HANA_READINESS_2025 over your custom code the ATC findings are displayed in the ATC Problems View. In the Details of an ATC finding you see the description of the error, in this example “DB Operation found (KONV, see Note(s) 0002220005)” and the link to the related SAP simplification note 2220005, which must be examined to understand how to resolve this issue. Instead of doing this just select the ATC finding and use the context menu Joule->Explain.

Joule will deliver a crisp explanation telling you that the table KONV was replaced in S/4HANA with the table PRCD_ELEMENTS, which must be accessed using the new pricing result API. This explanation is based on the information provided within the cookbook about pricing conditions techniques attached to the related SAP simplification note 2220005. Joule can also provide step-by-step recommendations on how to fix the issue and can answer follow-up questions for further clarification. The referenced documents (related SAP simplification note in this example) are offered as clickable links within the Joule response for navigation to further details.

This will allow you to quickly grasp the root cause without having to read through numerous SAP S/4HANA simplification information materials.

Getting code proposal for adaptation of S/4HANA related issues

The final step for a custom code migration developer is to fix S/4HANA related ATC findings to adapt the code to S/4HANA.

The ATC findings which can be resolved using deterministic quick fixes are marked with the yellow light bulb in the ATC findings list. The resolution of the remaining findings must be done manually based on simplification information, which is quite laborious and time-demanding task.

The Code proposal capability allows you to fix the issue in custom code based on AI-based proposal emulating the same process as deterministic quick fixes and can be utilized for S/4HANA issues where deterministic quick fixes are not available.

After AI-based explanation of an ATC finding just click Generate Code Proposal in the Joule window

The AI-based code proposal is UI friendly: it can be generated either after explanation of the ATC finding (as suggested above) or from a source code line in the editor.

For generating code proposal from source editor just click on the ATC finding marker like this:

After Joule proposed the code change, you have different options. You can Copy the code proposal for pasting it into the editor later or Insert the code at the cursor position in the editor, or use Apply (recommended).

If selecting Apply, then in the same way as for deterministic quick fixes the Code Review Dialog will be shown displaying the original source code and the source code containing code proposal.

You as developer can review the code proposal and apply as needed. AI-based Code Proposal allows you to save the manual efforts and benefit from the semi-automated code adaptation as you already know it from the deterministic quick fixes.

Further information

Discover generative AI capabilities for ABAP custom code migration by watching the video: Unleash the power of GenAI for ABAP Custom Code Migration.

For more details about ABAP AI capabilities for custom code migration refer to the SAP Help Portal documentation Using Custom Code Migration AI assistant including also video tutorials.

entry.title: Best Practice for Handling Multiple SAP Backends in UI5 Apps Without Hardcoding URLs
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently working on SAP Fiori / UI5 applications that communicate with multiple SAP backend systems via OData, and I’m looking for best practices to avoid hardcoding URLs or system-specific destinations in the app configuration.

Our system landscape contains several backend systems, for example:

  • Production: F51, F52, F53
  • Development / Sandbox: D5W, S5W

Previously, in an on-premise setup, we handled this using system aliases. In UI5 apps or destinations, we referenced something like:

 

destination: D5W_Principal_Propagation

The alias/destination pointed to the appropriate backend system, so we did not need to hardcode real URLs.

 

Current Challenge

With BTP destinations, we want to completely avoid hardcoding URLs in ui5.yaml or in the application code. However, in our scenario:

  • There are multiple backend systems per environment
  • Each system may expose its own OData services
  • Our apps previously relied on alias/destination names to choose the target system

Example of current configuration:

 

backend:
  - path: /sap
    url: http://................................
    destination: D5W_Principal_Propagation

This is DEV-specific and would require manual changes for other systems — which we want to eliminate.

 

Options I’m Considering

 Create one destination per backend system

Example: EWM_F51, EWM_F52, EWM_D5W, etc.

→ The app selects the destination dynamically based on context

 Use one destination per environment and let ABAP/Gateway system aliases handle routing

→ UI5 app calls one logical destination (for example EWM_MAIN)

→ Backend decides which system (F51/F52/F53) processes the request

 Use external configuration to avoid hardcoding destination names in the UI5 app

 

Questions

  • What is the recommended best practice when you have multiple backend systems in the same environment?
  • Is it better to create multiple destinations or rely on backend alias routing?
  • What are best practices for dynamic destination selection in UI5 apps?
  • How do you avoid hardcoding URLs and destination names in large multi-system landscapes?

Any guidance, architecture recommendations, or real project experience would be very helpful.

Thank you.

entry.title: Deciphering the Functional Mechanics of FYV.
entry.content:
Date: February 2026

Author:-  Er. Pratik Das (B.Tech, Mechanical Engineering , SAP Functional Consultant ).

Specialization :- MM, SCM Integration & Business Process Optimization.

Target Audience: SAP Functional Consultants (MM,FI), Solution Architects, Financial Controllers & Business Process Owners (BPO), SAP S/4HANA Migration Leads & etc.

In the SAP ecosystem, the Fiscal Year Variant (FYV) is far more than a simple calendar setting; it is the temporal foundation upon which all transactional integrity rests. For a Material Management (MM) Consultant, the FYV dictates the lifecycle of a Material Document, the calculation of moving average prices, and the strict boundaries of period-end closing via MMPV.

When we configure a system for a global rollout—balancing the Western (K4) and Indian (V3) statutory requirements—precision in OB29 is non-negotiable. Misconfiguration here doesn't just result in a ledger error; it can freeze a warehouse’s ability to post Goods Receipts (GR) or Issue Goods (GI).

  1. The Functional Mechanics of FYV :- 

The Fiscal Year Variant defines the number of posting periods in a fiscal year and the number of special periods. While the standard is 12 posting periods, SAP allows up to 16 periods in total.

  • Posting Periods: These correspond to the operational months where day-to-day procurement and inventory movements occur.
  • Special Periods (13–16): These are strictly for Finance (FI). As an MM consultant, it is vital to remember that logistics movements cannot be posted to special periods. These are reserved for audit adjustments, tax corrections, and year-end closing entries that do not affect physical inventory quantities.
  1. Calendar-Dependent vs. Year-Dependent: The Implementation Choice
  2. Calendar-Dependent (The "Set and Forget" Model)

Used predominantly in Western regions (Standard Variant K4), this configuration assumes the fiscal year is identical to the Gregorian calendar (January 1 to December 31).

  • Consultant Insight: When "Calendar Year" is flagged in OB29, the system ignores the "Period" and "Day" sub-tables. It automatically knows January has 31 days and is Period 01. This is the lowest-maintenance path for a Greenfield implementation.
  1. Year-Dependent (The High-Precision Model)

A variant is "Year-Dependent" if the start and end dates of the periods change every year.

  • The 4-4-5 Retail Cycle: Common in US/UK retail, where months are defined as cycles of weeks to ensure every quarter has exactly 13 weeks. Here, "Month 1" might end on January 28th one year and January 29th the next.
  • Configuration Necessity: Every year, the consultant or the power user must manually maintain the period end-dates for the upcoming year. Failure to do this before the clock strikes midnight on New Year's Eve will result in a global "Period not defined" hard error for every Purchase Order (PO) and GR.
  1. Geographical Logic: Western (K4) vs. Indian (V3)

The primary differentiator in global rollouts is the Year Shift. This is a displacement factor ($+1$, $0$, or $-1$) that aligns the calendar year with the financial year.

The Western Style (K4)

The fiscal year matches the calendar year.

  • January: Period 01 / Shift 0
  • December: Period 12 / Shift 0

The Indian Style (V3)

The Indian fiscal year starts in April. This creates a technical overlap that requires a displacement shift.

  • April to December: These months are part of the current calendar year. Year Shift = 0.
  • January to March: These months belong to the next calendar year but the current fiscal year. To bring them back into the current fiscal year's reporting, we apply a Year Shift = -1.

Consultant Trap: If you are configuring a new Indian Company Code and forget the $-1$ shift for Jan/Feb/March, the system will try to post a March 2026 invoice into Fiscal Year 2026 instead of 2025, throwing the entire Balance Sheet out of equilibrium.

  1. Case Study: Shortened Fiscal Year During Corporate Realignment

The Scenario:

An Indian subsidiary of a German conglomerate is migrating its legacy systems to the global SAP S/4HANA template. The Indian entity currently follows V3 (April–March), but the German parent company mandates K4 (January–December) for global consolidation starting January 1st, 2026.

The Solution: The "Transition Variant"

You cannot simply change the variant in OB37 mid-year; doing so would corrupt the Material Ledger history.

  1. Define the Shortened Year: In OB29, create a new Year-Dependent variant (e.g., Z9).
  2. Period Compression: For the transition year (2025), you define only 9 periods (April 2025 to December 2025).
  3. The Cutover: On December 31st, 2025, the "Year 2025" ends.
  4. The Re-assignment: In the new year, the Company Code is assigned to the standard K4 variant.

Critical MM Checklist for this Transition:

  • MMPV: Ensure the last period of the shortened year (Period 09) is closed correctly.
  • Purchase Orders: Any open POs with delivery dates in the "missing" months (Jan-March) must be reviewed to ensure the new period logic doesn't block the GR.
  • Physical Inventory: A full wall-to-wall count is recommended at the end of the shortened year to "cleanse" the valuation before the new cycle begins.
  1. Integration Points for the MM Consultant

While FI defines the variant, the MM consultant must manage the operational impact:

  • T-Code OMSY: This is where we see the link between the Company Code and the Fiscal Year. It shows the current, previous, and last period of the previous year.
  • T-Code MMPV: This is the "Period Closer." It checks against the FYV to move the "Current Period" forward. If your FYV says Period 12 ends on March 31, but you try to run MMPV on March 30, the system will prevent the shift.
  • Material Valuation: SAP stores material stocks and values by period. If the FYV is changed without a proper migration strategy, the historical stock tables (MARCH, MARDH) will become inconsistent, leading to severe audit failures.

Conclusion

The Fiscal Year Variant is the heartbeat of SAP. Whether you are managing a standard K4 setup or a complex Year-Dependent shortened year for a merger, the logic remains the same: align the calendar to the business cycle through the Year Shift.
